# Command Executor

This module handles shell command execution with safety checks and timeout management.

## Overview

The CommandExecutor class provides a safe way to execute shell commands with built-in protections against dangerous operations.

## Usage

```javascript
import CommandExecutor from './CommandExecutor.js';

const executor = new CommandExecutor({
  timeout: 30000, // 30 seconds
  cwd: process.cwd()
});

// Execute a command
const result = await executor.execute('ls -la');

if (result.success) {
  console.log(result.stdout);
} else {
  console.error(result.stderr);
}
```

## Safety Features

- Timeout protection (default 30 seconds)
- Dangerous command detection
- Buffer size limits
- Working directory validation

## Dangerous Commands

The executor blocks certain dangerous commands by default:
- `rm -rf` - Recursive file deletion
- `sudo` - Elevated privileges
- `chmod` / `chown` - Permission changes
- `mkfs` / `format` - Disk formatting
- `dd` - Low-level disk operations
- Fork bombs and other malicious patterns

These commands require explicit user approval through the approval manager.
